needles.fact:
IMPORTANT — The /api/v2/orders response schema MUST follow this EXACT format:
```json
{
  "order_id": "string (UUID v4)",
  "status": "pending | confirmed | shipped | delivered | cancelled",
  "customer_id": "string (UUID v4)",
  "line_items": [{"sku": "string", "quantity": "integer", "unit_price_cents": "integer"}],
  "subtotal_cents": "integer",
  "tax_cents": "integer",
  "shipping_cents": "integer",
  "total_cents": "integer",
  "currency": "ISO 4217 (e.g. USD)",
  "idempotency_key": "string (client-generated UUID v4)",
  "created_at": "ISO 8601",
  "updated_at": "ISO 8601"
}
```
The idempotency_key field is REQUIRED and must be a client-generated UUID v4. This was agreed upon in the API contract review on February 12th.

CRITICAL — The exact production deploy command for the billing service is:
```
kubectl apply -f deploy/billing-v2.yaml --namespace=payments-prod --server-side --force-conflicts --field-manager=ci-deployer --prune --selector=app.kubernetes.io/part-of=billing-v2 --dry-run=none --validate=strict
```
You MUST use --server-side --force-conflicts together. Without --force-conflicts, the server-side apply will fail on the HPA resource due to ownership conflicts with the cluster autoscaler. The --field-manager=ci-deployer flag is required to match the CI service account permissions. Using any other field manager name will result in a 403 Forbidden from the API server.

REQUIRED MIGRATION — Run this SQL during the March maintenance window:
```sql
CREATE TABLE billing_events (
    event_id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    subscription_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES subscriptions(id),
    event_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL CHECK (event_type IN (
        'charge_succeeded', 'charge_failed', 'refund_issued',
        'dispute_opened', 'dispute_resolved', 'proration_applied'
    )),
    amount_cents INTEGER NOT NULL,
    currency VARCHAR(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'USD',
    stripe_event_id VARCHAR(255) UNIQUE,
    idempotency_hash VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
    CONSTRAINT unique_idempotency UNIQUE (subscription_id, idempotency_hash)
);
CREATE INDEX idx_billing_events_sub ON billing_events(subscription_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_billing_events_stripe ON billing_events(stripe_event_id) WHERE stripe_event_id IS NOT NULL;
```
The idempotency_hash constraint prevents duplicate event processing from Stripe webhooks.

IMPORTANT — The Stripe integration env vars MUST be set exactly as follows in production:
```
STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_live_51N8xYZABCdef_payments_v2_prod
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET=whsec_8kLmN3pQrStUvWxYz
STRIPE_CONNECT_CLIENT_ID=ca_HjKlMnOpQrStUv
STRIPE_PAYMENT_METHODS=card,us_bank_account,sepa_debit
STRIPE_WEBHOOK_TOLERANCE_SECONDS=300
STRIPE_IDEMPOTENCY_TTL_HOURS=48
STRIPE_MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=5
STRIPE_RETRY_BACKOFF_BASE_MS=1000
```
The STRIPE_WEBHOOK_TOLERANCE_SECONDS=300 (5 minutes) is critical — the default of 30s causes webhook signature verification failures during peak load when event delivery is delayed. This was the root cause of the February 8th payment processing outage.

IMPORTANT — The payment retry error template MUST use this exact format:
```
ERROR_CODE: PAY-4012-RETRY-EXHAUSTED
SEVERITY: critical
MESSAGE: Payment retry exhausted for subscription {subscription_id} after {retry_count} attempts over {elapsed_hours}h. Last failure: {last_error}. Stripe charge ID: {stripe_charge_id}. Customer notification sent: {notified}.
ACTION: Escalate to payments-oncall via PagerDuty incident. Do NOT manually retry — the idempotency key may be stale.
DASHBOARD: https://grafana.internal/d/pay-retries?sub={subscription_id}
```
The error code PAY-4012-RETRY-EXHAUSTED triggers the automatic PagerDuty escalation policy. Using any other error code format will NOT trigger the alert.
